entities.name: Facebook, Inc.
entities.description: A social media company involved in a legal case regarding web scraping.

entities.name: Power Ventures, Inc.
entities.description: A company that was ruled against for scraping Facebook pages.

entities.name: Associated Press
entities.description: A news organization that held a legal case against Meltwater for scraping and republishing news.

entities.name: Meltwater U.S. Holdings, Inc.
entities.description: A company that was held liable for scraping news information from the Associated Press.

entities.name: hiQ Labs
entities.description: A company involved in a legal case against LinkedIn regarding web scraping.

entities.name: LinkedIn
entities.description: A professional networking site involved in a legal case regarding web scraping.

entities.name: Internet Archive
entities.description: An organization that collects and distributes publicly available web pages.

entities.name: Danish Maritime and Commercial Court
entities.description: A court that ruled on the legality of web scraping in Denmark.

entities.name: Ryanair
entities.description: An airline involved in a legal case regarding the enforceability of click-wrap agreements.

entities.name: Billigfluege.de GmbH
entities.description: A company involved in a legal case against Ryanair regarding web scraping.

entities.name: French Data Protection Authority (CNIL)
entities.description: The authority that released guidelines on web scraping in France.

entities.name: Spam Act 2003
entities.description: An Australian law that outlaws certain forms of web harvesting.

entities.name: Information Technology Act, 2000
entities.description: An Indian law that penalizes unauthorized access to computer resources.

relationships.description: Power Ventures was ruled against for scraping Facebook pages.
Meltwater was held liable for scraping and republishing news from the Associated Press.
hiQ Labs was involved in a legal case against LinkedIn regarding web scraping.
Internet Archive collects publicly available web pages without violating copyright laws.
The court ruled that systematic crawling and indexing by ofir.dk does not conflict with Danish law.
Ryanair's click-wrap agreement was ruled legally binding in a case against Billigfluege.de.
CNIL released guidelines clarifying that publicly available data is still personal data.
The Spam Act 2003 outlaws certain forms of web harvesting in Australia.
The act penalizes unauthorized access to computer resources in India.
